questions.question: Explain the difference between a class and an object with an example.
keyPoints: Class is a user-defined data type blueprint
Object is an instance of a class
Example: Class 'Car' defines attributes like color, speed and methods like drive(), brake()
Objects like myCar, yourCar are actual instances with specific values

questions.question: What are the three access specifiers in C++? Explain each briefly.
keyPoints: Public: accessible from anywhere
Private: accessible only within the class
Protected: accessible within class and derived classes
Default for class is private, for struct is public

questions.question: What is a constructor? List and explain different types of constructors.
keyPoints: Constructor is a special member function called automatically on object creation
Default constructor: takes no arguments
Parameterized constructor: takes arguments for initialization
Copy constructor: creates object as copy of another object
Move constructor: transfers resources from temporary object

questions.question: Explain the 'this' pointer with an example.
keyPoints: 'this' is an implicit pointer pointing to current object
Used to distinguish between member variables and parameters with same name
Available only in non-static member functions
Example: this->x = x assigns parameter x to member variable x

questions.question: What are static members in a class? Explain static data members and static member functions.
keyPoints: Static members belong to class, not to any object
Static data member: shared by all objects, initialized once, exists even without objects
Static member function: can be called without object, can only access static data
Accessed using class name: ClassName::staticMember

questions.question: What is the difference between constructor and destructor?
keyPoints: Constructor called when object is created, destructor when object is destroyed
Constructor name same as class, destructor has ~ prefix
Constructor can be overloaded, destructor cannot
Constructor initializes, destructor releases resources
Constructor has no return type, destructor also has no return type

questions.question: Explain the concept of encapsulation with an example.
keyPoints: Encapsulation bundles data and methods operating on that data
Restricts direct access to some components
Achieved using access specifiers (private data, public methods)
Example: BankAccount class has private balance, public deposit() and withdraw() methods

questions.question: Define a class 'Student' with data members (name, roll number, marks) and member functions to input and display student details. Also demonstrate how to create and use objects of this class.
keyPoints: Class definition with private data members
Public member functions: inputData(), displayData()
Member function definitions inside/outside class
Creating objects, calling functions
Showing sample output

questions.question: What is a copy constructor? When is it called? Write a C++ program to demonstrate copy constructor.
keyPoints: Copy constructor initializes object from another object
Called during: pass by value, return by value, explicit copy
Syntax: ClassName(const ClassName& obj)
Demonstrate shallow vs deep copy concept
Program showing copy constructor usage

questions.question: Explain friend functions and friend classes in C++. Write a program to demonstrate friend function accessing private members.
keyPoints: Friend function: non-member function with access to private members
Declared using friend keyword inside class
Friend class: all member functions of one class can access private members of another
Shows example program with friend function
Discuss advantages and disadvantages

questions.question: Write a C++ program to implement a class 'Complex' for complex numbers with constructor, and overloaded + operator as a member function to add two complex numbers.
keyPoints: Class definition with real, imaginary data members
Constructor for initialization
Operator overloading for +
Member function definition
Demonstration with main function
